([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Vlaams Gewest*, pronounced [[ˌvlaːms xəˈʋɛst]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Vlaams_Gewest.ogg "File:Nl-Vlaams Gewest.ogg")),[[a]](#cite_note-5)[[b]](#cite_note-fn1-6) usually simply referred to as **[Flanders](/wiki/Flanders "Flanders")** (Dutch: *Vlaanderen* [[ˈvlaːndərə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Vlaanderen.ogg "File:Nl-Vlaanderen.ogg")),[[c]](#cite_note-fn2-7) is one of the three [regions](/wiki/Communities,_regions_and_language_areas_of_Belgium "Communities, regions and language areas of Belgium") of [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium")—alongside the [Walloon Region](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia") and the [Brussels-Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels").[[5]](#cite_note-Belgian_Constitution-8) Covering the northern portion of the country, the Flemish Region is [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")-speaking. With an area of 13,626 km2 (5,261 sq mi), it accounts for only 45% of Belgium's territory, but 58% of its population. It is one of the most densely populated regions of Europe with around 500/km2 (1,300/sq mi).

The **Flemish Region** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Vlaams Gewest*, pronounced [[ˌvlaːms xəˈʋɛst]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Vlaams_Gewest.ogg "File:Nl-Vlaams Gewest.ogg")),[[a]](#cite_note-5)[[b]](#cite_note-fn1-6) usually simply referred to as **[Flanders](/wiki/Flanders "Flanders")** (Dutch: *Vlaanderen* [[ˈvlaːndərə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Vlaanderen.ogg "File:Nl-Vlaanderen.ogg")),[[c]](#cite_note-fn2-7) is one of the three [regions](/wiki/Communities,_regions_and_language_areas_of_Belgium "Communities, regions and language areas of Belgium") of [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium")—alongside the [Walloon Region](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia") and the [Brussels-Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels").[[5]](#cite_note-Belgian_Constitution-8) Covering the northern portion of the country, the Flemish Region is [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")-speaking. With an area of 13,626 km2 (5,261 sq mi), it accounts for only 45% of Belgium's territory, but 58% of its population. It is one of the most densely populated regions of Europe with around 500/km2 (1,300/sq mi).

The Flemish Region is distinct from the [Flemish Community](/wiki/Flemish_Community "Flemish Community"): the latter encompasses both the inhabitants of the Flemish Region and the Dutch-speaking inhabitants of the [Brussels-Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"). It borders the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") and [France](/wiki/France "France").

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flemish_Region&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Politics")]

Immediately after its establishment in 1980, the region transferred all its constitutional competencies to the [Flemish Community](/wiki/Flemish_Community "Flemish Community"). Thus, the current Flemish authorities ([Flemish Parliament](/wiki/Flemish_Parliament "Flemish Parliament") and [Flemish Government](/wiki/Flemish_Government "Flemish Government")) represent all the [Flemish people](/wiki/Flemish_people "Flemish people"), including those living in the [Brussels-Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels"). Hence, the Flemish Region is governed by the Flemish Community institutions. However, members of the Flemish Community parliament elected in the Brussels-Capital Region have no right to vote on Flemish regional affairs.

## Administrative divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flemish_Region&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Administrative divisions")]

Further information: [Provinces of Flanders](/wiki/Provinces_of_Flanders "Provinces of Flanders")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bd/Provinces_of_Flanders.svg/250px-Provinces_of_Flanders.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Provinces_of_Flanders.svg)

Provinces in the Flemish Region

The Flemish Region comprises five [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Belgium "Provinces of Belgium"), each consisting of [administrative arrondissements](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Belgium#Administrative "Arrondissements of Belgium") that, in turn, contain [municipalities](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") (in total 285 municipalities in Flanders).

The seat of the Flemish parliament is located in Brussels, which is an [enclave](/wiki/Enclave_and_exclave "Enclave and exclave") within – but not part of – the Flemish region, being specified that the [Brussels-Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels "Brussels") is established as an administrative region of Belgium in its own right. In contrast, the Walloon parliament has established its parliament in the territory of [Wallonia](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia"), specifically in the city of [Namur](/wiki/Namur "Namur"), [Namur Province](/wiki/Namur_Province "Namur Province").

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flemish_Region&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

Flanders is home to a diversified modern economy, with emphasis put on research and development. Many enterprises work closely with local knowledge and research centres to develop new products and services.[[6]](#cite_note-9) The [Gross domestic product](/wiki/Gross_domestic_product "Gross domestic product") (GDP) of the region was €270 billion in 2018, accounting for 59% of Belgium's economic output. GDP per capita adjusted for purchasing power was €36,300 or 136% of the EU27 average in the same year.[[7]](#cite_note-10)

### Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flemish_Region&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Transport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ab/Van_Hool_bus_Mechelen2.JPG/250px-Van_Hool_bus_Mechelen2.JPG)](/wiki/File:Van_Hool_bus_Mechelen2.JPG)

Bus of "De Lijn"

"[De Lijn](/wiki/De_Lijn "De Lijn")" serves as the main public transport company, run by the Flemish government. It consists of buses and trams. [TEC](/wiki/TEC_(transport) "TEC (transport)") is the equivalent company in Wallonia, and [MIVB-STIB](/wiki/Brussels_Intercommunal_Transport_Company "Brussels Intercommunal Transport Company") in Brussels. The railway network run by the [NMBS](/wiki/NMBS "NMBS"), however, is a federal responsibility.

The Flemish government is also responsible for all the highways (900 kilometers) and the 500 kilometers of other regional roads (Dutch: *gewestwegen*) in the territory of the region. Other types of roads are municipal roads.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d0/Fietssnelwegen_van_Vlaanderen.svg/250px-Fietssnelwegen_van_Vlaanderen.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Fietssnelwegen_van_Vlaanderen.svg)

The network of the Flemish cycle highways, some continuing into Brussels and Wallonia.

The network of [bicycle highways](/wiki/Bicycle_highway "Bicycle highway") (Dutch: *fietssnelweg*) in Flanders is being expanded under the responsibility of the provinces. Combined with the [Brussels Region](/wiki/Brussels_Region "Brussels Region"), this network has 2,700 kilometres of bicycle highways.[[8]](#cite_note-11)

The region is served by these airports:

* [Brussels Airport](/wiki/Brussels_Airport "Brussels Airport")
* [Antwerp International Airport](/wiki/Antwerp_International_Airport "Antwerp International Airport")
* [Ostend–Bruges International Airport](/wiki/Ostend%E2%80%93Bruges_International_Airport "Ostend–Bruges International Airport")

### Language

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flemish_Region&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Language")]

The official language of the Flemish Region is [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language").[[10]](#cite_note-constitution-13) The [dialect cluster](/wiki/Dialect_cluster "Dialect cluster") spoken in the region is sometimes colloquially referred to as Flemish (*Vlaams*),[[11]](#cite_note-14)[[12]](#cite_note-15)[[13]](#cite_note-Omniglot-16) Flemish Dutch (*Vlaams-Nederlands*), Belgian Dutch (*Belgisch-Nederlands*), or Southern Dutch (*Zuid-Nederlands*). Spelling and grammar are regulated by a single authority, the [Dutch Language Union](/wiki/Dutch_Language_Union "Dutch Language Union") (*Nederlandse Taalunie*), comprising a committee of ministers of the Flemish and Dutch governments, their advisory council of appointed experts, a controlling commission of 22 parliamentarians, and a secretariat.[[14]](#cite_note-Taalunie1-17)[[15]](#cite_note-Taalunie2-18) The term [Flemish](/wiki/Flemish_dialects "Flemish dialects") can be applied to the Dutch spoken in Flanders; it shows many regional and local variations.[[16]](#cite_note-19) The main dialect groups include [West Flemish](/wiki/West_Flemish "West Flemish"), [East Flemish](/wiki/East_Flemish "East Flemish"), [Brabantian](/wiki/Brabantian_dialect "Brabantian dialect") and [Limburgish](/wiki/Limburgish "Limburgish").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0f/Arrondissement_Brussels-Periphery_Belgium_Map.PNG/250px-Arrondissement_Brussels-Periphery_Belgium_Map.PNG)](/wiki/File:Arrondissement_Brussels-Periphery_Belgium_Map.PNG)

The municipalities with language facilities near Brussels

French (specifically [Belgian French](/wiki/Belgian_French "Belgian French")) may also be used in the Flemish Region for certain administrative purposes in a limited number of the so-called "[municipalities with language facilities](/wiki/Municipalities_with_language_facilities "Municipalities with language facilities")" around the Brussels-Capital Region and on the border with [Wallonia](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia"). These "rim municipalities" around Brussels are [Drogenbos](/wiki/Drogenbos "Drogenbos"), [Kraainem](/wiki/Kraainem "Kraainem"), [Linkebeek](/wiki/Linkebeek "Linkebeek"), [Sint-Genesius-Rode](/wiki/Sint-Genesius-Rode "Sint-Genesius-Rode"), [Wemmel](/wiki/Wemmel "Wemmel") and [Wezembeek-Oppem](/wiki/Wezembeek-Oppem "Wezembeek-Oppem"). Brussels was originally a Dutch-speaking city (Brabantian dialect to be exact),[[17]](#cite_note-tokyo-20) but it was [francised](/wiki/Francisation_of_Brussels "Francisation of Brussels") in the 19th and 20th centuries and is now officially bilingual in French and Dutch[[10]](#cite_note-constitution-13) (although largely French-speaking in practice).[[18]](#cite_note-rudi3-21) Municipalities with language facilities on the border with Wallonia are [Bever](/wiki/Bever,_Belgium "Bever, Belgium") (French: *Biévène*), [Herstappe](/wiki/Herstappe "Herstappe"), [Mesen](/wiki/Mesen "Mesen") (French: *Messines*), [Ronse](/wiki/Ronse "Ronse") (French: *Renaix*), [Spiere-Helkijn](/wiki/Spiere-Helkijn "Spiere-Helkijn") (French: *Espierres-Helchin*), and [Voeren](/wiki/Voeren "Voeren") (French: *Fourons*).

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flemish_Region&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Religion")]

Religion in the Flemish Region in 2016[[19]](#cite_note-ULB-22)

1. [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Belgium "Catholic Church in Belgium") (68.0%)
2. [Protestantism](/wiki/Protestantism "Protestantism") (2.00%)
3. [Irreligion](/wiki/Irreligion "Irreligion") (26.0%)
4. [Islam](/wiki/Islam "Islam") (2.00%)
5. Other religion (2.00%)

According to a 2016 survey by the [Free University of Brussels](/wiki/Universit%C3%A9_libre_de_Bruxelles "Université libre de Bruxelles"), 68% of Flemish citizens are [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Roman_Catholic "Roman Catholic"), 2% are [Protestant](/wiki/Protestant "Protestant"), 26% are irreligious, while 2% have other religions.[[19]](#cite_note-ULB-22)
